revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:1 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:1月5日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 1월 5일]][[wuu:正月初五]][[zh-min-nan:1 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:正月初五]][[zh:正月初五]]
{{Not to be confused with|January 5|qq=January 5}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=一月初五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 1월 5일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar January|qq=}} '''January 5''' is the 5th day of the month {{ill|Zhengyue|en|Zhengyue|qid=Q1072983|lt=January (Mutsuki)|1=Zhengyue}}, the 1st month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Taian|qid=Q96759844|1=taian}}{{efn|ja|大安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taian|1=taian|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1306255}}.
== Events == * [[成務天皇]] 1st year （{{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|131|en|131|qid=Q395788|lt=131|1=131}} {{ill|February 18|en|February 18|qid=Q2343|lt=February 18|1=February 18}}） – The 13th Emperor, Emperor Seimu, acceded to the throne. * [[仁賢天皇]] 1st year （{{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|488|en|488|qid=Q31811|lt=488|1=488}} {{ill|February 4|en|February 4|qid=Q2318|lt=February 4|1=February 4}}） – The 24th Emperor, Emperor Ninken, acceded to the throne. * {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|675|en|675|qid=Q30523|lt=675|1=675}} {{ill|February 8|en|February 8|qid=Q2323|lt=February 8|1=February 8}}） – Emperor Tenmu established Japan’s first astronomical observatory to observe celestial phenomena and determine auspiciousness. * {{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|qid=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|824|en|824|qid=Q30353|lt=824|1=824}} {{ill|February 12|en|February 12|qid=Q2336|lt=February 12|1=February 12}}） – The era name was changed from Kōnin to Tenchō. * [[:en:貞和|貞和]] 4th year /{{ill|Shōhei|en|Shōhei|qid=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1348|en|1348|qid=Q6085|lt=1348|1=1348}} {{ill|February 4|en|February 4|qid=Q2318|lt=February 4|1=February 4}}） – Kusunoki Masayuki, the son of Kusunoki Masashige, was defeated and committed suicide after fierce fighting with General Takashina. * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 1st year （{{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1592|en|1592|qid=Q6769|lt=1592|1=1592}} {{ill|February 17|en|February 17|qid=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}） – Toyotomi Hideyoshi issued the order to march, sending troops via Korea to Ming, initiating the Imjin War. * {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1855|en|1855|qid=Q7673|lt=1855|1=1855}} {{ill|February 21|en|February 21|qid=Q2350|lt=February 21|1=February 21}}） – The Tokugawa shogunate ratified and exchanged the treaty document of the Japan–US Treaty of Amity and Commerce with American envoy Adams.
== Births ==
* [[道光]] 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1823|en|1823|qid=Q6988|lt=1823|1=1823}} {{ill|February 15|en|February 15|qid=Q2334|lt=February 15|1=February 15}}） – Li Hongzhang, a politician of Qing China (d. {{ill|1901|en|1901|qid=Q2035|lt=1901|1=1901}}). * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|qid=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1863|en|1863|qid=Q7701|lt=1863|1=1863}} {{ill|February 22|en|February 22|qid=Q2351|lt=February 22|1=February 22}}） – Tsuboi Masagorō, an anthropologist (d. {{ill|1913|en|1913|qid=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|qid=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1867|en|1867|qid=Q7715|lt=1867|1=1867}} {{ill|February 9|en|February 9|qid=Q2324|lt=February 9|1=February 9}}） – Natsume Sōseki, a novelist (d. {{ill|1916|en|1916|qid=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}}).
== Deaths == * [[:en:貞和|貞和]] 4th year /{{ill|Shōhei|en|Shōhei|qid=Q1143587|lt=Shōhei|1=Shōhei}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1348|en|1348|qid=Q6085|lt=1348|1=1348}} {{ill|February 12|en|February 12|qid=Q2336|lt=February 12|1=February 12}}） – Kusunoki Masayuki, a military commander and son of Kusunoki Masashige (born {{ill|1326|en|1326|qid=Q5782|lt=1326|1=1326}}). * 貞和 4th year / 正平 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} 1348, February 12) – Kusunoki Masatoki, a military commander and the younger brother of Kusunoki Masayuki. * {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1580|en|1580|qid=Q6712|lt=1580|1=1580}} {{ill|January 21|en|January 21|qid=Q2273|lt=January 21|1=January 21}}） – Kitabatake Tomofusa, the legitimate son of Kitabatake Toshinori (born {{ill|1547|en|1547|qid=Q6547|lt=1547|1=1547}}). * [[Bunroku]]{{efn|Period of Japanese history (1592–1596)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunroku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1593|en|1593|qid=Q6772|lt=1593|1=1593}} {{ill|February 6|en|February 6|qid=Q2320|lt=February 6|1=February 6}}） – Emperor Shōchin, the 106th Emperor of Japan (born {{ill|1517|en|1517|qid=Q6263|lt=1517|1=1517}}).
* {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|qid=Q1146135|lt=Keian|1=Keian}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1651|en|1651|qid=Q6956|lt=1651|1=1651}} {{ill|February 24|en|February 24|qid=Q2355|lt=February 24|1=February 24}}） – Mōri Hideyoshi, the 2nd lord of the Chōshū Domain (born {{ill|1595|en|1595|qid=Q6782|lt=1595|1=1595}}). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 1st year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1804|en|1804|qid=Q6894|lt=1804|1=1804}} {{ill|February 15|en|February 15|qid=Q2334|lt=February 15|1=February 15}}） – Takahashi Shijirō, an astronomer and teacher of Ino Tadataka (born {{ill|1764|en|1764|qid=Q7659|lt=1764|1=1764}}). * {{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|qid=Q776775|lt=Keiō|1=Keiō}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} {{ill|January 29|en|January 29|qid=Q2286|lt=January 29|1=January 29}}） – Inoue Genzaburō, the commander of the 6th unit of the Shinsengumi (born {{ill|1829|en|1829|qid=Q7572|lt=1829|1=1829}}). * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} {{ill|February 15|en|February 15|qid=Q2334|lt=February 15|1=February 15}}） – Yokoi Koson, a Confucian scholar and thinker (born {{ill|1809|en|1809|qid=Q6919|lt=1809|1=1809}}).
== Commemorations ==
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|1|4|1|6|12|5|2|1|0105|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|1月5日 (旧暦)|version=103740832|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[cdo:1 nguŏk 5 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:January 5 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 1월 5일]] [[ja:1月5日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:正月初五]]
[[ar:January 5 (Lunisolar)]]
<!-- The lunisolar and Gregorian calendars do not align exactly --><!-- Note: [[高山右近]] is recorded as Keichō 20th year, January 8 --> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[wuu:正月初五]] [[zh-min-nan:1 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:正月初五]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[wuu:正月初五]] [[zh-min-nan:1 goe̍h 5 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:正月初五]] {{wikidata link|Q835425}} [[Category:Days 5 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar January]] [[Category:Taian]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]
